changes_made: Renamed k8s_audit_log_integrity_verification_enabled to k8s_audit_log_integrity_verified for clarity and consistency
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control SC-16(1) by focusing on integrity and secure transmission of data. The naming convention mostly follows the established pattern, but a minor adjustment was made to improve clarity and consistency. The functions are technically accurate and align with Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Consider using 'verified' instead of 'verification_enabled' to indicate a clear pass/fail state
title: Transmission Of Security And Privacy Attributes - Transmission of security and privacy attributes | integrity verification verify
step1_reasoning: The control SC-16(1) focuses on ensuring the integrity of security and privacy attributes during transmission. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to several areas: ensuring secure transport for network communications, encrypting data at rest and in transit (e.g., etcd encryption), and ensuring integrity of audit logs. NetworkPolicies can enforce secure transport, etcd encryption ensures data integrity at rest, API server TLS ensures secure communication, and audit log integrity verification aligns with ensuring audit logs are not tampered with.
